title: Keep selinux mount label when privileged also
body:
Set the SELiux mount label for privileged containers
as well as they are applied to mounts and rootfs.
Only the process label should be wiped out.

Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: @rhatdan but then you will be calling `label.InitLabels()` twice - that is why I was doing it the way in the PR. I can change it to do that if you believe that is better.

NO you will only call it in parseCreaeOpts if it is not priv, and call it directly when it is. 

BTW, I am reworking all of this stuff now, do you want me to take this over.

I have added the fix for this to https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1406
So I am going to close this PR.
